title: Full Day Electric Bike Tour
description: Get ready for white sand beaches, meditation gardens, canyon hiking, unique stops, and local history. Also included are tasty restaurant options for lunch. You can enter or exit this tour at any time. If you leave out the hike, this tour can be completed in a half day. The tour is a cell phone, narrated, guided tour. The map on your phone will show you the stops and the route we recommend. It will also show your position along the route, so you won't get lost. When you get close to a scheduled stop, your app tour guide will share information, history, and stories with you. Do not forget your ear buds or earphones for the best audio enjoyment. The route is on bike lanes and on residential streets. You will cover about 20 miles with 16 stops including one with a hike through Annie's Canyon. This experience cannot be fully enjoyed in less than a day, so please plan to start in the morning and enjoy the entire day. Fun in the sun, good exercise, incredible views, walking gardens, and local history. If you need to rent an electric bike for the tour, we recommend our bike rental partner, Pedego Solana Beach. Their rental bikes come with a phone holder on the handlebar for your phone and a standard USB port to keep your phone charged from the bike's power source. Bring your phone charging cord to connect with the bike. Also included is a helmet, lock, and bottle of drinking water. You can contact them at 858.764.2979 or make an on line reservation at pedegosolanabeach.com

title: 10% Off Mon-Thurs Intimate Whale Watching Tours
description: Experience the thrill of being up close to nature on a small, fast, rigid inflatable boat! We glide through the ocean on a military decommissioned boat on a wildlife safari to see whales, dolphins, sea-lions, and so much more! Our boats are low profile which allows us to get arms reach away from wildlife. On our educational, guided tours every passenger gets to enjoy 360-degree, front row views and one-on-one time to talk to the captain. With no crowds, no hassle, and a personalized tour, there is no question why Adventure Whale Watching has been given 5-star ratings by almost every explorer! With small group sizes we are able to personalize every tour to give each of our guests their own unique and memorable experience! Join us for the adventure of a lifetime! Make your reservation today!Adventurewhalewatchingsandiego.com(619) 808-2822 Who we are: At Adventure Whale Watching we specialize in fast-moving, fun boat tours on Navy Seal Rigid Inflatable Boats (R.I.B.s). We are one of the only year-round Whale & Dolphin Watching companies in San Diego! Our boats set us apart because they are low profile which creates stability so it eliminates the aggressive rocking that you experience on the bigger, slow-moving boats. Our boats are also faster than typical whale watching tours which means that we are able to cover more area allowing us to see more and get closer to the wildlife. We have a small number of passengers per boat which will ensure that the boat is never crowded and that each of the guests gets a personalized experience with 360 degree views from every seat! Educational: Our captains are also naturalists and marine biologists who will educate you about the wildlife, the boats, and give you some history about San Diego! We have NOAA Marine Mammal Identification Guides we use to help passengers learn about and identify the potential species we can see on the tour. We also have tide charts and Boating Safety Information. We also talk about the Marine Mammal Protection Act, Entanglements, and Environmental Conservation. We may even pick up some balloons or water bottles if we find garbage in the ocean to help our marine friends!Safety: Our tours are guided by United States Coast Guard certified captains and naturalists aboard military decommissioned Rigid Inflatable Boats (R.I.B.s). These low-profile boats are some of the safest boats on the water! Additionally, we offer each passenger a life vest and the choice of a snow suit or a military survival suit to keep them warm and dry while on the boat. Join Us! Winter excursions, between December 15th & March 31st, are offered daily at 8:30am, 11:30am, and 2:30pm. Summer excursions, between April 1st & October 14th, are offered daily at 8:30, 12:00pm, & 3:30pm. Fall excursions, between October 15th & December 14th, are offered daily at 8:30 & 12:00pm. Pricing: The price for 2.5 - 3 hour Public Whale Watching tours are $129 plus tax per guest. Private Whale Watching tours are offered for $775 plus tax for up to 6 passengers. Use code WEEKDAY10 for 10% Monday - Thursday tours! Please call to accommodate groups larger than 6. Reservations are required.

title: Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific Exhibit
description: Opening Saturday, May 23 • Memorial Day Weekend 2026Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific opens at the Maritime Museum of San Diego and is included with general admission. Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific explores the deep and often overlooked relationships between Black communities and the Pacific Ocean. Spanning centuries of maritime movement, labor, exploration, and cultural exchange, the exhibit highlights the lives and contributions of Black mariners whose stories helped shape the Pacific world. Visitors are invited to discover the experiences of sailors, whalers, fishers, waterfront workers, explorers, soldiers, surfers, swimmers, and scholars whose connections to the ocean expanded far beyond traditional narratives of maritime history. Through immersive storytelling, historical interpretation, and contemporary scholarship, the exhibit offers a broader and more inclusive understanding of the Pacific—revealing how identity, labor, migration, resilience, and culture moved across oceans and generations. Developed in partnership with UC San Diego curator Dr. Caroline Collins, Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific brings together research, storytelling, and maritime history to explore Black relationships with the Pacific Ocean and the broader maritime world. The exhibit invites visitors to reconsider familiar narratives of exploration, labor, and seafaring through stories that have too often remained outside traditional maritime histories.

description: Enjoy an unbeatable happy hour with bites starting at $2, plus beer & cocktail specials.

title: Electric Bike/Train Coastal Tour
description: Want to ride the rails and explore on your electric or road bike? The tour begins at the Solana Beach Train Station where you will board the train, with your bike, headed to Carlsbad Village. The train fare of $5 is included in your tour charge. The train follows the coastline, so you can enjoy spectacular views along the way. Once off the train in Carlsbad, your bike adventure will begin. As you ride back to Solana Beach you will enjoy incredible coastline vistas, white sand beaches, meditation gardens, local history, and restaurants to experience for lunch. The bike riding leg of the tour is about 16 miles with up to 14 stops along the way. This tour is a cell phone, narrated and guided tour app. The map on your phone will show you the stops and the route we recommend. It will also show your position along the route, so you won't get lost. When you get close to a scheduled stop, your app tour guide will share information, history, and stories with you. Do not forget your ear buds or earphones for the best audio enjoyment. If you need to rent an electric bike for the tour please contact Pedego Solana Beach at 858.764.2979 or you can make an online reservation at pedegosolanabeach.com. Their rental bikes come with a cell phone holder on the handlebar and a standard USB port to keep your phone charged from the bike's power source. Bring your phone charging cord to connect with the bike. The bike rental is not included in the tour cost. This experience should not be rushed, so plan to start in the morning and enjoy the entire day. Fun train ride, incredible coastline views, tasty food, no traffic or parking hassles, and fun in the sun. Most of the tour you will be riding in a bike lane on a road with automobiles.

title: La Jolla Tour - Sip, Savor and Sea
description: The Sip, Savor and Sea tour is a great way to explore the coastal town of La Jolla with a professional tour guide. La Jolla is known as the "Jewel of San Diego" with some much marine wildlife, art, and historic figures who have paved the way in the world of science and medicine. Learn about their stories, enjoy seaside views, and walk along the village streets lined with galleries and trendy restaurants. What to Expect: A guided walking tour of La Jolla Village & Cove, weaving together scenic oceanfront views, local history, culinary tastings, and art. The walk covers roughly ½ to ¾ mile, including a downhill segment to reach the Cove—so comfy shoes are a must. Culinary & Culinary Highlights: Enjoy a savory food and drink tasting at a local restaurant, often featuring gourmet tacos and margaritas—Puesto is one favored stop. Wrap up on a sweet note with dessert, typically artisanal gelato from a local French/Italian-style bakery. Cultural and Scenic Stops: Visit an art gallery, led by a curator, offering a glimpse into the local contemporary art scene. Stroll past iconic landmarks like Sunny Jim's Sea Cave, a quirky and historic cave accessed via the famous Cave Store. At the picturesque La Jolla Cove, spot sea lions basking on rocks and take in sweeping ocean views. Storytelling & Local Flavor: Guides share engaging stories about La Jolla's history, with a special nod to pioneering figures—especially women—who helped shape the area's rich cultural and scientific landscape. You'll also hear fun local anecdotes—about Hollywood-era hangouts, marine mysteries, and more.

title: You Should Know Podcast Presents: The YSK HOUSE Tour
Madhushree Ghosh - Safar
description: Warwick’s presents Madhushree Ghosh as she discusses and signs her new book, Safar: Finding Home, History, and Culture Through Punjabi Food in the American West. Madhushree Ghosh is the daughter of refugees and an immigrant to America. She is also the author of the award-winning Khabaar: An Immigrant Journey of Food, Memory, and Family. She uses food to discuss civil rights, social justice, culinary-, community-, and cultural history. Ghosh’s work has appeared in Best American Essays in Food Writing, been Pushcart nominated, and published in The New York Times, Vogue India, The Washington Post, Los Angeles Times, Lit Hub, The Rumpus, and Atlas Obscura, among other outlets. She runs the global literary salon and super club KhabaarCo, enabling conversations on food cultures, writing, travel, and mindful activism through conversations with changemaking activists, historians, authors, and chefs. Ghosh considers both San Diego and New Delhi home.

title: I Listened To The Wind
description: Becky Savell’s exhibit explores the connection between the feminine form and nature, using flora as a symbol of endurance and transformation. The show features a mix of photography, silkscreen, painting, fabric, and print, creating a layered and immersive visual experience. You can visit the exhibit during scheduled hours from June 3 to June 27, 2026, at the Poway Center for the Performing Arts.

description: Embrace the classics at the Mainly Mozart Festival in San Diego, CA, where leading musicians of the top orchestras and chamber ensembles convene to produce several unforgettable concerts. If you love stunning harmonies, then meander to the Mainly Mozart Festival.

title: The Most Happy Fella
description: The Most Happy Fella is a lush, romantic musical masterpiece with music, lyrics, and libretto by Frank Loesser. Set in California’s Napa Valley, it tells a passionate tale of unexpected love, featuring soaring melodies and vibrant Broadway showstoppers. The production is directed by Jeffrey B. Moss and features a heartfelt, classic score by the Pulitzer Prize–winning composer of Guys and Dolls.

title: The Monsters
description: For years, LIL has observed her estranged brother from the shadows. BIG, a force in the mixed martial arts circuit, remains unaware his sister has been tracking his career… until she appears unexpectedly at his door. Award-winning playwright Ngozi Anyanwu writes and stars alongside Sullivan Jones (HBO's The Gilded Age) in this warm-hearted sibling story exploring how to love each other better, and the fight to build a life you can be proud of.

title: The Monsters
description: The Monsters is a West Coast-premiere play by Ngozi Anyanwu, directed by Tamilla Woodard with choreography by Adesola Osakalumi. The story follows siblings Lil and Big, exploring their tumultuous childhood and present-day reunion through the lens of Mixed Martial Arts. The play combines poetry and movement to depict a gritty and emotional love story between estranged siblings.

title: The Art of Dr. Seuss Featured in a New Show, "San Diego, Dr Seuss Lived Here"
description: It's Summer in Dr. Seuss's Backyard, San Diego Coming to Exclusive Collections Gallery, Gaslamp, for a Limited Engagement The Summer begins in the Gaslamp Quarter by celebrating American Icon Dr. Seuss, ahead of a newly discovered Cat in the Hat book to be released in June on the 250th Anniversary of the Declaration of Independence called "Sing the 50 United States." There are two cities synonymous with Dr. Seuss: Springfield, Massachusetts, where he was born, and San Diego, where he perfected his craft as a children's book author and artist. Dr. Seuss lived in La Jolla from 1948 until his passing in 1991. From UCSD, to the San Diego Library and Scripps Green Hospital Geisel Patient Care Pavillion, Dr. Seuss's imprint is all over San Diego - and it is seen in the art. Now you can enjoy and even collect it too. Much of Dr. Seuss's work was inspired by the breathtaking coastline of California, especially San Diego. Now you can see some of his works of art, never seen before, at a new show "San Diego, Dr Seuss Lived Here" running May 29 - June 29 at Exclusive Collections (EC Gallery) in the Gaslamp Quarter at 621 5th Ave. Downtown San Diego. The newest secret art release from the collection, "Small Bird, Lots of Feathers" will be prominently displayed. Other highlights include: "I Dreamed I Was a Doorman at the Hotel del Coronado" and the whimsical and popular story and art of The Lorax. Pieces in the exhibition are available for acquisition, some with rare availability. Each limited edition has been carefully adapted and reproduced from Geisel's original paintings, drawings, or sculptures and bears an authorized Dr. Seuss Estate signature, making this collection both a rare opportunity and a celebration of an American icon's artistic legacy. "We have been honored to be the exclusive gallery representing The Art of Dr. Seuss Collection on the West Coast for many years," said Ruth-Ann Thorn, Founder/CEO, EC Gallery. "Being a native San Diegan, it is wonderful see first-hand and share these tremendous drawings, paintings, sculptures, many inspired by San Diego, with our city and art collectors near and far to enjoy." Audrey Geisel, the late widow of Theodor Seuss Geisel, once remarked, "I'm gratified to carry out Ted's wishes and have these works revealed to the world." Visitors to the exhibition will experience exactly that, a window into the imagination of one of the 20th century's most creative minds. NOW through June 29.

title: Chair Yoga
description: This event offers a functional movement practice focusing on flexibility, strength building, and mental quieting. Participants can join at their own ability level and are encouraged to wear flexible clothing and bring preferred yoga equipment. Chairs are provided, and the event is suitable for adults of all ages with no pre-registration required.

title: San Diego County Fair
description: "Once Upon A Fair" in a land not-so-far-away, the story of your San Diego summer begins. The San Diego County Fair is the greatest story our community has ever told. It's a tale of food, fun, friends, family, laughter, tradition, music, amazement, and pure, unbridled joy. The Fair — the biggest and longest-running event in San Diego County — makes its triumphant return from Wednesday, June 10, through Sunday, July 5, 2026 (closed Mondays and Tuesdays). This year's theme, "Once Upon A Fair" invites Fair fanatics to write a new chapter: one about the narratives that shaped us, the simple pleasures of good books, the power of storytelling, and all the magic found between the lines. Our featured theme exhibit invites fairgoers of all ages to explore the world of books through playful photo ops, educational exhibits, and hands-on fun. Tell Your Story: Discover your inner author! Join workshops on writing, illustrating, and publishing your own fictional or biographical tale. Golden Books: Step into the treasured stories of your youth—from classic fairy tales to young adult adventures—and rediscover the magic that made you fall in love with reading. Color & Comics: Explore the bold, vibrant world of comics, graphic novels, and giant coloring books—because not all great stories are told in words. Library Love: Learn how libraries have shaped communities, past and present, and how you can unlock their hidden treasures. Book Fair: Browse our Book Fair for your next summer read. Mystery, Sci-Fi, Romance or Travel? There's something for every bookworm to devour! But there is much more to this story. Throughout the Fairgrounds you'll choose your own adventure: Hunger Games: Feast on fair-favorite foods — from classic corn dogs and candied apples to wildly imaginative (and deep-fried!) culinary creations. Goosebumps: Brave more than 70 thrilling rides in the Fun Zone and Kids Zone —twists, turns, and stomach-flipping surprises await! Lord of the Rings: Test your skills at our carnival games for a chance to win your own precious prize. Where the Wild Things Are: Meet barnyard buddies in the Petting Farm, learn about livestock animals raised for food and fiber, and catch edu-tainment shows starring furry, fluffy, and feathered (and often funny!) friends. Treasure Island: Explore a treasure trove of handcrafted goods, unique finds, and curious trinkets in our bustling shopping halls—your perfect souvenir might just be the next great plot twist in your day! You won't want to miss your chance to live out your "happily ever after" at the 2026 San Diego County Fair.

title: The Spongebob Musical
description: Join SpongeBob SquarePants and all your favorite Bikini Bottom characters as they come to life on a stage near you. Featuring an original score by a legendary lineup of Grammy Award-winning writers, this show will take you on a hilarious and heartwarming adventure.

title: San Diego County Fair
description: The San Diego County Fair is a beloved summer tradition that has entertained visitors since 1880. Held annually, it offers an exciting blend of agricultural exhibits, competitive displays in arts and hobbies, thrilling rides, and games. With over 60 attractions, live concerts, and interactive experiences, it’s a vibrant celebration of local culture and family fun. Whether exploring livestock barns or enjoying the fair’s signature delights, there’s something for everyone.

title: Toyota Summer Concert Series
description: Experience unforgettable nights at the Toyota Summer Concert Series in San Diego, where the Corona Grandstand Stage at Del Mar Fairgrounds comes alive with music as the sun sets over the Pacific. This celebrated series brings together an exciting mix of legendary acts and rising stars, offering something for every music fan. With the iconic Ferris Wheel lighting up the fairgrounds, guests enjoy top-tier performances in a lively, festive atmosphere. Whether you’re a longtime attendee or a first-time visitor, the event promises memorable entertainment in a truly unique setting.

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to Juan Gabriel on Strings
description: This event is a Candlelight concert featuring a string quartet performing the music of Juan Gabriel. It takes place at the Fleet Science Center in San Diego and offers a unique multi-sensory musical experience under candlelight. The performance lasts 60 minutes, with doors opening 45 minutes before the show.

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to The Beatles
description: Candlelight: A Tribute to The Beatles is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing iconic songs by The Beatles. The event takes place at the Fleet Science Center in San Diego and offers a 60-minute concert under candlelight. It is designed for audiences aged 8 and older, with assigned seating and ADA accessibility.

title: Candlelight: Best of Joe Hisaishi
description: Candlelight: Best of Joe Hisaishi is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing iconic pieces from Joe Hisaishi's anime soundtracks. The event takes place at the Fleet Science Center in San Diego and offers a 60-minute performance under candlelight. The program includes themes from popular films such as My Neighbour Totoro, Spirited Away, and Princess Mononoke.

title: A Bad Case of Stripes: The Musical
description: This musical adaptation of David Shannon’s book follows Camilla, who struggles to balance her love for her favorite food with the desire to fit in. As she tries to conform, she becomes increasingly miserable—and more colorful. The show conveys a message about the importance of embracing one’s true self in the journey toward self-discovery and acceptance.

title: FIFA® World Cup 2026 Experience at LEGOLAND® California Resort
description: Build. Dribble. Score! Kick off the ultimate family day out at the FIFA® World Cup 2026 Experience, arriving at LEGOLAND® California Resort this June for a limited time only! Share a piece of the action at this high-energy soccer celebration packed with interactive games, epic LEGO brick builds and experiences inspired by LEGO soccer heroes. Take on the challenge and score against a LEGO minifigure, then experience Spot Shot and Super Squad activities. Get ready for fan favorite experiences including:​ Meet Soccer Legends in LEGO® brick form - get up close to brick-built soccer icons​ Design Your Winning Jersey - build your dream jersey in LEGO bricks and add it to the Heroes of Play Wall.​ LEGO FIFA® World Cup Trophy - celebrate your victory with an iconic photo moment It's a goal-scoring, brick building event full of teamwork, fun and unforgettable moments. Book now, before the final whistle blows!

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to Metallica
description: Candlelight: A Tribute to Metallica is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing Metallica's music in a candlelit setting. The event takes place at the San Diego Natural History Museum and lasts approximately 60 minutes. It offers an immersive atmosphere with assigned seating and is suitable for audiences aged 8 and older.

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to Pink Floyd
description: Candlelight: A Tribute to Pink Floyd is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing Pink Floyd's music under candlelight. The event takes place at the San Diego Natural History Museum and lasts approximately 60 minutes. It offers an immersive atmosphere with assigned seating and is suitable for audiences aged 8 and older.

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to Fleetwood Mac
description: Candlelight: A Tribute to Fleetwood Mac is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing Fleetwood Mac's music in a candlelit setting. The event takes place at the San Diego Natural History Museum and lasts approximately 60 minutes. It offers an intimate atmosphere with assigned seating and is accessible to attendees aged 8 and older.

title: SDMA 100 Years
description: SDMA 100 Years is a multi-day exhibition at the San Diego Museum of Art that presents photographs, ephemera, and film footage documenting key moments in the museum's history. The exhibition traces the museum's evolution from its origins at the 1915 Panama–California Exposition through its grand opening in 1926, its use as a naval hospital during World War II, and its development through the late 20th century to a reimagined future. It highlights the museum's engagement with the community and its cultural heritage over the past century.

title: "The Guardian - Wings of Sacrifice" Art Installation
description: Liberty Station is proud to feature a public art installation created in collaboration with Retired Navy Chief and acclaimed artist Joe Pisano, supported by the Resurrecting Lives Foundation. Featuring Pisano's work "The Guardian - Wings of Sacrifice," the installation will serve as a visual tribute honoring the U.S. Navy's 250 years of service, courage and sacrifice. Displayed within the Arts District at Liberty Station, the piece is made from 2,500 military dog tags and other creative materials. The installation will include a commemorative plaque featuring a QR code linking to Liberty Station's signature NTC Alumni Video series, inviting visitors to hear firsthand accounts from veterans who trained on property during its time as the NTC. Joe Pisano's art exhibition will be in Liberty Station's Command Center, 2640 Historic Decatur Road, San Diego, CA 92106. This piece will be viewable daily: Mondays through Saturdays from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. and Sundays from 11 a.m. to 5 p.m.

title: Parks After Dark
description: Parks After Dark is a free summer series that transforms neighborhood parks into lively hubs of activity, entertainment, and connection. From movie nights under the stars to live music, games, and community resources, there's something for everyone. Example activities: Music & Entertainment Community Resources Arts & Crafts Inflatables Youth Sports Clinics Health & Fitness Kids eat FREE while supplies last. Thursday, Friday, and Saturday nights from June 5th to August 1st. Five locations at Rec Centers across San Diego: City Heights: 4380 Landis St. San Diego, CA 92105 Linda Vista: 7064 Levant St. San Diego, CA 92111 Memorial: 2975 Ocean View Blvd. San Diego, CA 92113 Skyline Hills: 8285 Skyline Dr. San Diego, CA 92114 Silverwing: 3737 Arey Dr. San Diego, CA 92154 Presented by: San Diego Parks Foundation, City of San Diego, County of San Diego, US Bank, Albertsons Foundation, Price Philanthropies, and Prebys Foundation.
