title: Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific Exhibit
description: Opening Saturday, May 23 • Memorial Day Weekend 2026Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific opens at the Maritime Museum of San Diego and is included with general admission. Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific explores the deep and often overlooked relationships between Black communities and the Pacific Ocean. Spanning centuries of maritime movement, labor, exploration, and cultural exchange, the exhibit highlights the lives and contributions of Black mariners whose stories helped shape the Pacific world. Visitors are invited to discover the experiences of sailors, whalers, fishers, waterfront workers, explorers, soldiers, surfers, swimmers, and scholars whose connections to the ocean expanded far beyond traditional narratives of maritime history. Through immersive storytelling, historical interpretation, and contemporary scholarship, the exhibit offers a broader and more inclusive understanding of the Pacific—revealing how identity, labor, migration, resilience, and culture moved across oceans and generations. Developed in partnership with UC San Diego curator Dr. Caroline Collins, Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific brings together research, storytelling, and maritime history to explore Black relationships with the Pacific Ocean and the broader maritime world. The exhibit invites visitors to reconsider familiar narratives of exploration, labor, and seafaring through stories that have too often remained outside traditional maritime histories.

title: Happy Hour at the Coop!
description: Enjoy an unbeatable happy hour with bites starting at $2, plus beer & cocktail specials.

title: Electric Bike/Train Coastal Tour
description: Want to ride the rails and explore on your electric or road bike? The tour begins at the Solana Beach Train Station where you will board the train, with your bike, headed to Carlsbad Village. The train fare of $5 is included in your tour charge. The train follows the coastline, so you can enjoy spectacular views along the way. Once off the train in Carlsbad, your bike adventure will begin. As you ride back to Solana Beach you will enjoy incredible coastline vistas, white sand beaches, meditation gardens, local history, and restaurants to experience for lunch. The bike riding leg of the tour is about 16 miles with up to 14 stops along the way. This tour is a cell phone, narrated and guided tour app. The map on your phone will show you the stops and the route we recommend. It will also show your position along the route, so you won't get lost. When you get close to a scheduled stop, your app tour guide will share information, history, and stories with you. Do not forget your ear buds or earphones for the best audio enjoyment. If you need to rent an electric bike for the tour please contact Pedego Solana Beach at 858.764.2979 or you can make an online reservation at pedegosolanabeach.com. Their rental bikes come with a cell phone holder on the handlebar and a standard USB port to keep your phone charged from the bike's power source. Bring your phone charging cord to connect with the bike. The bike rental is not included in the tour cost. This experience should not be rushed, so plan to start in the morning and enjoy the entire day. Fun train ride, incredible coastline views, tasty food, no traffic or parking hassles, and fun in the sun. Most of the tour you will be riding in a bike lane on a road with automobiles.

title: La Jolla Tour - Sip, Savor and Sea
description: The Sip, Savor and Sea tour is a great way to explore the coastal town of La Jolla with a professional tour guide. La Jolla is known as the "Jewel of San Diego" with some much marine wildlife, art, and historic figures who have paved the way in the world of science and medicine. Learn about their stories, enjoy seaside views, and walk along the village streets lined with galleries and trendy restaurants. What to Expect: A guided walking tour of La Jolla Village & Cove, weaving together scenic oceanfront views, local history, culinary tastings, and art. The walk covers roughly ½ to ¾ mile, including a downhill segment to reach the Cove—so comfy shoes are a must. Culinary & Culinary Highlights: Enjoy a savory food and drink tasting at a local restaurant, often featuring gourmet tacos and margaritas—Puesto is one favored stop. Wrap up on a sweet note with dessert, typically artisanal gelato from a local French/Italian-style bakery. Cultural and Scenic Stops: Visit an art gallery, led by a curator, offering a glimpse into the local contemporary art scene. Stroll past iconic landmarks like Sunny Jim's Sea Cave, a quirky and historic cave accessed via the famous Cave Store. At the picturesque La Jolla Cove, spot sea lions basking on rocks and take in sweeping ocean views. Storytelling & Local Flavor: Guides share engaging stories about La Jolla's history, with a special nod to pioneering figures—especially women—who helped shape the area's rich cultural and scientific landscape. You'll also hear fun local anecdotes—about Hollywood-era hangouts, marine mysteries, and more.

title: Happy Hour
description: Available weekdays at our bar and front dining room! Featuring deals on wine and sangria, along with our best-selling burger, truffle parm papas, hummus, and croquettes. *Happy Hour food specials are subject to change.*

description:
From the city where they sun shines through the mist, Temecula, California, comes the jazzy folk duo Nathan & Jessie.

Born of the individuals, Nathan Rivera and Jessie Andra Smith, this duo is a unique blend of jazzy folk and blues on resonator guitars and accordion, along with interesting interplay between male and female vocals. Often joined on tour and on recordings by their friends, Trevor Mulvey on Upright Bass, and Blake Armstrong on Drums.

title: Argentine Tango Class
description: All levels, all ages class taught by Eduardo Santana every Monday. There is a fee of $20 per class. If you would like to attend a class, wear shoes that allow for pivoting.

title: Barbara McQuade - The Fix
description: Warwick’s presents Barbara McQuade as she discusses and signs her new book, The Fix: Saving America from the Corruption of a Mob-Style Government, in conversation with Carol Lam. Barbara McQuade is a professor from practice at the University of Michigan Law School, her alma mater, where she teaches courses in criminal law, criminal procedure, national security, and data privacy. She is also a legal analyst for NBC News and MSNBC, and co-host of the #SistersInLaw podcast. From 2010 to 2017, McQuade served as the US Attorney for the Eastern District of Michigan. She was appointed by President Barack Obama and was the first woman to serve in her position. Earlier in her career, she worked as a sportswriter and copy editor, a judicial law clerk, an associate in private practice, and an assistant US attorney. McQuade is author of the national bestseller, Attack from Within: How Disinformation is Sabotaging America. She and her husband have four children and live in Ann Arbor, Michigan.

title: Blue Monday
description: Every Monday. 9 PM NO Cover!! Resident DJ’S Camilla Robina and Faux Velvet He will be playing an all-vinyl set event highlighting genres ranging from post-punk to glam rock to […]

title: Blue Monday
description: Every Monday. 9 PM NO Cover!! Resident DJ’S Camilla Robina and Faux Velvet He will be playing an all-vinyl set event highlighting genres ranging from post-punk to glam rock to […]

title: Desarrollo Infantil Temprano: ¿Cuándo, Dónde, Cómo y Por Qué?
description: Una conversación comunitaria para familias y cuidadores

title: TRIVIA NIGHT | WILD HARE BAR GARDEN
description: Test your knowledge at Trivia Night in Wild Hare Bar Garden Lounge—every Tuesday at 6 PM. Half-off taco plates during trivia! Put your knowledge to the test at Trivia Night at Wild Hare Bar Garden Lounge, happening every Tuesday at 6:00 PM at the Hilton San Diego Gaslamp Quarter. Presented by Trivia Goat and hosted in partnership with Walkabout, this weekly series brings together friendly competition, great drinks, and Gaslamp energy. Kicking off February 3, grab your crew and settle in for a lively night of trivia, laughs, and midweek fun. Enjoy half-off taco plates during trivia, plus an exclusive Walkabout Insider perk—the first 10 Walkabout Insiders each week will receive a rotating complimentary perk. Free to play. Prizes, bragging rights, and good times encouraged.

title: Don Toliver
description: Don Toliver Octane Tour is a concert featuring American rapper, singer, and songwriter Don Toliver. The tour supports his fifth studio album OCTANE, released in 2026, which blends melodic trap and R&B with cinematic and immersive themes. The event includes performances by SahBabii, SoFaygo, and Chase B as supporting artists.

title: Don Toliver
description: Don Toliver is a Houston-based hip-hop artist who has rapidly become one of the most unique acts on the scene. His soulful melodic vibes have made him one of the most sought-after voices in modern hip-hop. Don Toliver has even earned major accolades for his work, including Billboard Music Award nominations for his collaboration with Travis Scott and recognition from XXL, Vibe, and Vulture. With his signature sound and critical praise, Don Toliver is here to stay.

title: Kiefer Sutherland
description: Kiefer Sutherland is a British-Canadian actor, director, producer and singer born in London, England. His most popular character is Jack Bauer from the drama series 24 for which he won the Emmy, Golden Globe and SAG awards. He also starred in movies like A Few Good Men, Phone Booth and Pompeii.

title: Chair Yoga
description: This event offers a functional movement practice focusing on flexibility, strength building, and mental quieting. Participants can join at their own ability level and are encouraged to wear flexible clothing and bring preferred yoga equipment. Chairs are provided, and the event is suitable for adults of all ages with no pre-registration required.

title: The Spongebob Musical
description: Join SpongeBob SquarePants and all your favorite Bikini Bottom characters as they come to life on a stage near you. Featuring an original score by a legendary lineup of Grammy Award-winning writers, this show will take you on a hilarious and heartwarming adventure.

title: San Diego County Fair
description: The San Diego County Fair is a beloved summer tradition that has entertained visitors since 1880. Held annually, it offers an exciting blend of agricultural exhibits, competitive displays in arts and hobbies, thrilling rides, and games. With over 60 attractions, live concerts, and interactive experiences, it’s a vibrant celebration of local culture and family fun. Whether exploring livestock barns or enjoying the fair’s signature delights, there’s something for everyone.

title: Toyota Summer Concert Series
description: Experience unforgettable nights at the Toyota Summer Concert Series in San Diego, where the Corona Grandstand Stage at Del Mar Fairgrounds comes alive with music as the sun sets over the Pacific. This celebrated series brings together an exciting mix of legendary acts and rising stars, offering something for every music fan. With the iconic Ferris Wheel lighting up the fairgrounds, guests enjoy top-tier performances in a lively, festive atmosphere. Whether you’re a longtime attendee or a first-time visitor, the event promises memorable entertainment in a truly unique setting.

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to Juan Gabriel on Strings
description: This event is a Candlelight concert featuring a string quartet performing the music of Juan Gabriel. It takes place at the Fleet Science Center in San Diego and offers a unique multi-sensory musical experience under candlelight. The performance lasts 60 minutes, with doors opening 45 minutes before the show.

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to The Beatles
description: Candlelight: A Tribute to The Beatles is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing iconic songs by The Beatles. The event takes place at the Fleet Science Center in San Diego and offers a 60-minute concert under candlelight. It is designed for audiences aged 8 and older, with assigned seating and ADA accessibility.

title: Candlelight: Best of Joe Hisaishi
description: Candlelight: Best of Joe Hisaishi is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing iconic pieces from Joe Hisaishi's anime soundtracks. The event takes place at the Fleet Science Center in San Diego and offers a 60-minute performance under candlelight. The program includes themes from popular films such as My Neighbour Totoro, Spirited Away, and Princess Mononoke.

title: A Bad Case of Stripes: The Musical
description: This musical adaptation of David Shannon’s book follows Camilla, who struggles to balance her love for her favorite food with the desire to fit in. As she tries to conform, she becomes increasingly miserable—and more colorful. The show conveys a message about the importance of embracing one’s true self in the journey toward self-discovery and acceptance.

title: FIFA® World Cup 2026 Experience at LEGOLAND® California Resort
description: Build. Dribble. Score! Kick off the ultimate family day out at the FIFA® World Cup 2026 Experience, arriving at LEGOLAND® California Resort this June for a limited time only! Share a piece of the action at this high-energy soccer celebration packed with interactive games, epic LEGO brick builds and experiences inspired by LEGO soccer heroes. Take on the challenge and score against a LEGO minifigure, then experience Spot Shot and Super Squad activities. Get ready for fan favorite experiences including:​ Meet Soccer Legends in LEGO® brick form - get up close to brick-built soccer icons​ Design Your Winning Jersey - build your dream jersey in LEGO bricks and add it to the Heroes of Play Wall.​ LEGO FIFA® World Cup Trophy - celebrate your victory with an iconic photo moment It's a goal-scoring, brick building event full of teamwork, fun and unforgettable moments. Book now, before the final whistle blows!

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to Metallica
description: Candlelight: A Tribute to Metallica is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing Metallica's music in a candlelit setting. The event takes place at the San Diego Natural History Museum and lasts approximately 60 minutes. It offers an immersive atmosphere with assigned seating and is suitable for audiences aged 8 and older.

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to Pink Floyd
description: Candlelight: A Tribute to Pink Floyd is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing Pink Floyd's music under candlelight. The event takes place at the San Diego Natural History Museum and lasts approximately 60 minutes. It offers an immersive atmosphere with assigned seating and is suitable for audiences aged 8 and older.

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to Fleetwood Mac
description: Candlelight: A Tribute to Fleetwood Mac is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing Fleetwood Mac's music in a candlelit setting. The event takes place at the San Diego Natural History Museum and lasts approximately 60 minutes. It offers an intimate atmosphere with assigned seating and is accessible to attendees aged 8 and older.

title: SDMA 100 Years
description: SDMA 100 Years is a multi-day exhibition at the San Diego Museum of Art that presents photographs, ephemera, and film footage documenting key moments in the museum's history. The exhibition traces the museum's evolution from its origins at the 1915 Panama–California Exposition through its grand opening in 1926, its use as a naval hospital during World War II, and its development through the late 20th century to a reimagined future. It highlights the museum's engagement with the community and its cultural heritage over the past century.

title: "The Guardian - Wings of Sacrifice" Art Installation
description: Liberty Station is proud to feature a public art installation created in collaboration with Retired Navy Chief and acclaimed artist Joe Pisano, supported by the Resurrecting Lives Foundation. Featuring Pisano's work "The Guardian - Wings of Sacrifice," the installation will serve as a visual tribute honoring the U.S. Navy's 250 years of service, courage and sacrifice. Displayed within the Arts District at Liberty Station, the piece is made from 2,500 military dog tags and other creative materials. The installation will include a commemorative plaque featuring a QR code linking to Liberty Station's signature NTC Alumni Video series, inviting visitors to hear firsthand accounts from veterans who trained on property during its time as the NTC. Joe Pisano's art exhibition will be in Liberty Station's Command Center, 2640 Historic Decatur Road, San Diego, CA 92106. This piece will be viewable daily: Mondays through Saturdays from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. and Sundays from 11 a.m. to 5 p.m.

title: Nighttime Zoo
description: Experience the magic of Nighttime Zoo at the San Diego Zoo, where the adventure continues into the evening with live music, dazzling entertainment, and unique wildlife encounters. Perfect for families, this beloved summer tradition invites guests to explore the zoo after hours and enjoy a lively atmosphere filled with unforgettable moments. After more than 25 years as a favorite seasonal event, Nighttime Zoo is preparing for its final season before a new festival debuts in the future. Don’t miss your chance to be part of this memorable celebration and create lasting memories with loved ones.

title: Nighttime Zoo
description: Activities and entertainment begin at 3:30 p.m. daily** As the day winds down, your unforgettable adventure is only just beginning. During Nighttime Zoo, the San Diego Zoo is open until 8 p.m. Swing by for live music, spectacular entertainment, and one-of-a-kind moments with your favorite wildlife that's perfect for the whole family. Join us all summer long to soak up the last season of Nighttime Zoo. You've celebrated with us for more than 25 years, and the curtains are closing on this family-favorite experience. But there's good news! Debuting in 2027, we're excited to welcome a brand-new seasonal festival—so get ready to make new generational memories over and over again! *Nighttime Zoo opens to all guests May 23. **No Nighttime Zoo entertainment is scheduled for Saturday, June 13 or Thursday, July 9. Activities include: -Rock & Roar: The Zoo's in-house rock and pop band plays top hits from yesterday and today that are sure to get everyone on the dance floor. -Zoolamoves: Join our high-energy hosts as they get guests of all ages up and grooving! These fun, interactive dances will have your toes tapping and energy pumping. -The Chameleons: This talented brass ensemble presents their unique brand of musical entertainment, playing a variety of songs from the Beatles to Broadway in their interactive performances. -Whimsical Wildlife Characters: Meet, greet, and snap photos with our fanciful, larger-than-life wildlife puppets and other wildlife-themed performers that are sure to bring the smiles. -Dr. Harry & Friends: Dr. Harry Lion and Friends will meet and greet guests of all ages, bringing their special brand of Zoo fun to everyone they see. -Jambo Dunia!: Live music, fanciful wildlife puppets, dancers, and acrobats make up this dazzling, interactive celebration of Africa's savannas and all who share these vibrant ecosystems. -Dr. Zoolittle's Exploration Station: Join Dr. Zoolittle for this fun and interactive presentation exploring nature, parallel play with wildlife, and our conservation efforts around the globe. -The Explorers: Enjoy the a cappella four-part harmony of this talented group of vocalists as they sing wildlife-themed tunes about the world we share. -Summer Songs: After a long day of exploration, relax with the soothing sounds of acoustic guitar and vocals as our performer entertains with a wide variety of songs from across the decades. -Bounce-a-Roo Bonanza: Check out our jumping, bouncing, acrobatic group of wildlife-themed performers as they show off their astonishing athletic skills in this high-energy trampoline show. -Amazing Acrobats of Tanzania: With an incredible mix of eclectic acrobatic skills and high-energy fun for all ages, this troupe from Tanzania will share jaw-dropping talent that will leave you amazed. -Vibrant Rhythms: Feel the rhythm of drumbeats as musicians and dancers showcase music from across East Africa.

title: Giants: Art from the Dean Collection of Swizz Beatz and Alicia Keys
description: Giants is a special exhibition showcasing a world-class collection of paintings, photographs, sculptures, and installations by nearly 40 Black diasporic artists. The collection is owned by musicians and producers Swizz Beatz and Alicia Keys, who have supported artists through their acquisitions. Organized by the Brooklyn Museum, the exhibition highlights Black diasporic artists from the Dean Collection.

title: Candlelight: A Tribute to Drake
description: Candlelight: A Tribute to Drake is a live musical experience featuring a string quartet performing Drake's music in a candlelit setting. The event takes place at the Fleet Science Center in San Diego and lasts approximately 60 minutes. It offers an intimate atmosphere with assigned seating and is suitable for audiences aged 8 and older.

title: TGIF Concerts in the Parks
description: TGIF Concerts in the Parks in Carlsbad, CA, is San Diego County’s longest-running free outdoor concert series, drawing thousands of attendees each summer. Families and friends gather on the grass with picnic blankets to enjoy live music, dance, and explore pop-up art booths in a welcoming, community-focused setting. The event is known for its lively atmosphere and diverse musical acts, making it a beloved tradition for locals and visitors alike. With a reputation for quality entertainment and a family-friendly environment, TGIF Concerts in the Parks is a highlight of Carlsbad’s cultural calendar.

title: Summer Spectacular
description: The Summer Spectacular at San Diego SeaWorld offers a vibrant mix of live entertainment, seasonal food and drinks, and a nightly drone show. Visitors can enjoy exciting shows, special summer-only attractions, and extended park hours for more aquatic adventures. It’s a high-energy celebration perfect for families looking to make the most of summer by the sea.

title: Summer Cocktail Series
description: Summer just got a whole lot tastier! Liberty Public Market's Summer Cocktail Series is back for YEAR THREE at Mess Hall Bar, shaking things up from May 22-Sep 7 Indulge in drinks inspired by: Loma Salad Bar, Bopjo, Cecilia's Taqueria, Asado Gaucho, Le Parfait Paris, Baker & Olive, Sangucheria, Mini Donut Company, Landini's Pizzeria Come sip your way through the market, one cocktail at a time.

title: Old Town Food Tour - Tequila, Tacos and Tombstones
description: This tour delivers a flavorful blend of culinary indulgence, cultural immersion, and slightly spooky entertainment—all wrapped up in a lively walk through San Diego's historic heart. Whether you're a history buff, foodie, or curious explorer, you're bound to leave with memorable stories (and a happy appetite)! What to Expect A vibrant 2.5-hour walking tour that immerses you in both the rich history and culinary delights of Old Town—celebrated as the "Birthplace of California." You'll explore locally significant sites, savor authentic Mexican cuisine, sip tequila cocktails, and dive into eerie ghost stories. Highlights Historical Journey: Discover how Old Town evolved through Native American life, early Spanish settlements, Mexican Independence, and the Gold Rush era—all while your guide brings these eras to life with engaging stories. Haunted Attractions: Pass by spooky landmarks including El Campo Santo Cemetery and the infamous Whaley House, dubbed "America's most haunted house." Tasty Tastings: Sample food at three local restaurants, including hearty tacos, salsas, and freshly made tortillas. Enjoy a Mexican dessert at a fourth stop. Sip on tequila-based cocktails (not shots)—often margaritas or specialty tequila mixes—accompanied by live mariachi music. Local Insights: Guides share insider recommendations for the best taco spots, plus regional history and folklore.

title: Gaslamp Quarter Food Tour - Brothels, Bites and Booze
description: The Brothels, Bites & Booze food tour in the Gaslamp Quarter is a quirky, fun, and historical culinary adventure through one of San Diego's most vibrant neighborhoods. This tour combines the rich history of the area with a sampling of delicious food and craft beverages. Here's a breakdown of what you can expect: 1. Historical Insight: The Gaslamp Quarter was once notorious for its seedy past, with brothels, saloons, and gambling halls operating in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. The tour highlights the history of this area, touching on the stories of vice and the characters who frequented the streets. Guides often share intriguing tales of the Wild West, lawlessness, and colorful personalities that shaped the area. 2. Delicious Food Stops: The "bites" part of the tour focuses on sampling a variety of local foods. Expect to try a mix of upscale and casual dishes from some of the best eateries in the Gaslamp Quarter. These stops might include bites like freshly made tacos, savory small plates, or seafood delicacies, depending on the tour's route. 3. Craft Cocktails and Local Brews: Of course, no tour of the Gaslamp would be complete without a little booze! You'll sip on craft cocktails, local beers, or perhaps a glass of wine while hearing about the area's storied past. Some stops might feature historical speakeasies or modern cocktail lounges where expert mixologists craft unique drinks. 4. Vibrant Atmosphere: The Gaslamp Quarter itself is a lively and eclectic mix of old Victorian buildings and modern establishments. As you stroll through the streets, you'll get to soak in the ambiance, from neon lights to ornate architecture, all while learning about the evolution of the neighborhood. 5. Unique Experience: It's a mix of history, culture, and flavor—offering a little something for everyone: history buffs, foodies, and drink enthusiasts alike. Along the way, you'll be entertained with stories about the area's famous residents, notorious events, and the evolution of the neighborhood. The Brothels, Bites & Booze tour is an interactive, lighthearted experience that offers a memorable blend of food, drink, and San Diego's colorful past. Whether you're a local or a visitor, it's a fun way to explore the Gaslamp Quarter's rich history and indulge in its culinary scene.

title: Little Italy Food Tour - Pizza, Past and Piazzas
description: The "Pizza, Pasta & Piazzas" food and drink walking tour is a vibrant 2½-hour culinary experience in the heart of San Diego's Little Italy. You'll wander through charming piazzas, sample standout Italian fare, and soak up stories about the neighborhood's rich history. Duration & ScheduleThe tour runs for about 2.5 hours, typically starting at 4:30 p.m. at the Piazza della Famiglia—a gathering hub centered around a fountain. Tasting StopsYou'll visit four handpicked spots, enjoying generous portion samples rather than mere nibbles. About two of them include wine or beer, and the final stop offers dessert. Culinary HighlightsThe menu may include: Fresh pappardelle Bolognese paired with wine (red or white) A slice of local, house-made Italian pizza A slider served with sangria or local beer A scoop of authentic gelato to round things off Cultural EnrichmentYour guide will share tales from Little Italy's past—its transformation from a bustling Italian immigrant fishing community to today's lively cultural and culinary district. You'll gain insight into the area's urban evolution, including connections to the early fishing and tuna canning industries. Local ConnectionThis isn't just about food—it's about the people behind it. The tour encourages engaging with shop owners and restauranteurs, offering a personal, authentic experience.

title: The Green of West Plaza: 90+ Years of Coronado Lawn Bowling Club
description: On View Now through September 13, 2026 The Green of West Plaza: 90+ Years of The Coronado Lawn Bowling Club celebrates over 90 years of the Coronado Lawn Bowling Club (CLBC) with an emphasis on its history, community involvement, and contribution to the sport of lawn bowling. The bilingual English/Spanish exhibit features trophies and medals won by members of the CLBC, highlighting the skill and sportsmanship that women and men have excelled at both on a national and international level. To complement the successes of the club, the exhibit explains the game of lawn bowls along with the equipment used. Very little equipment is necessary to play bowls, making it an accessible sport for all demographics. Lawn bowling is one of the few sports that allows women and men to compete together on an equal platform regardless of age.

title: Tuesday Night Potluck
description: Tuesday Night Potluck is a recurring open mic comedy event held at The Comedy Store in La Jolla. The event creates a phone-free space for artists and comedians using Yondr bags to enhance the live experience. Doors open at 6:45 PM and the show starts at 7:00 PM every Tuesday through December 29, 2026.

title: Clearly Indigenous
description: Clearly Indigenous: Native Visions Reimagined in Glass is an exhibition showcasing contemporary Native American and indigenous Pacific-Rim artists working in glass. The exhibition features approximately 120 glass art objects created by twenty-nine Native American artists, four Pacific Rim artists from New Zealand and Australia, and glass artist Dale Chihuly. It explores how Indigenous artists fuse heritage and contemporary glassmaking to illuminate their stories, connection to nature, and cultural traditions.

title: Cafés and Cabarets: The Spectacular Art of Toulouse-Lautrec
description: This exhibition showcases approximately fifty works by Henri de Toulouse-Lautrec from the permanent collection of The San Diego Museum of Art. It highlights his portraits of Paris nightlife and the cabaret culture of Belle Époque Paris, featuring his innovative color lithographs. The exhibition runs from April 4 to September 20, 2026, and celebrates the museum’s centennial, along with a significant gift from the Baldwin Foundation.

title: Candlelight: Coldplay and Imagine Dragons
description: Candlelight concerts create a unique atmosphere by combining live music with the warm glow of candlelight in stunning venues. Experience the music of Coldplay and Imagine Dragons performed by the Range Ensemble string quartet at the San Diego Natural History Museum. The intimate setting and carefully curated program offer a fresh way to enjoy these iconic songs.

title: India Thompson: Looks Like Home
description: India Thompson reflects on ownership and emotional attachment through intricately woven reed sculptures, transforming everyday domestic objects into meditations on home in the age of renting. The exhibition explores the emotional connections people form with ordinary domestic objects in spaces they call home. Thompson’s sculptures use basketry to tell stories and highlight the importance of craft across cultures.

title: Old Town Haunted History & Paranormal Investigation
description: Hear the Stories, Then Hunt for the Ghosts. This isn't your average ghost tour. Our most popular Old Town adventure is a unique, two-part experience that takes you from skeptic to investigator. First, you'll walk the haunted streets and hear the chilling history from our master storytellers. Then, you'll become a real paranormal investigator, using professional equipment to hunt for the very ghosts you just learned about. Your Two-Part Adventure Part 1: The Haunted History Tour Exclusive Access Where Other Tours Can't Go: Our journey takes you through the infamous Whaley House grounds and grants exclusive, after-dark entry into both the El Campo Santo Cemetery and the famously haunted Heritage Park. You won't just hear the stories; you'll stand in the very places where they happened. Master Storytelling: Hear the true, chilling tales of the spirits and legends that make Old Town San Diego one of the most haunted places in America, brought to life by our expert local guides. Part 2: The Paranormal Investigation Use Real Ghost-Hunting Equipment: The stories are over—now it's your turn. We'll arm you with and teach you how to use professional gear like EMF meters, spirit boxes, and dowsing rods to detect and communicate with the other side. Learn Beginner Techniques: Our expert investigators will guide you through the basics of conducting a real paranormal investigation. You'll learn how to ask questions, interpret evidence, and protect yourself from spiritual energy—no experience necessary! Is This Tour For You? This tour is perfect for the curious and the adventurous who want a hands-on experience. Due to the spooky themes and use of equipment, it is recommended for guests ages 10 and up. Ready to become a ghost hunter? Book your investigation now.

title: The Gaslamp Quarter Ghost Tour
description: Sin, Scandal, and Spirits in San Diego's Party Central Beneath the glittering lights and rooftop bars of the Gaslamp Quarter lies a sinister history of sin, scandal, and spirits who never left. This is not a gentle stroll; it's a fast-paced, 2-hour walking tour that dives deep into the district's sordid past, uncovering chilling tales of true crime, notorious brothels, and infamous serial killers. What You'll Experience Exclusive Access Inside Haunted Hotspots: Go beyond the velvet ropes where other tours can't. We'll take you inside the historic Davis-Horton House, a former hospital where many drew their last breath, and the notoriously haunted Horton Grand Hotel, where the ghost of an infamous gunslinger still plays tricks on guests. A Focus on Dark History & True Crime: This is our darkest tour. Hear the shocking, historically accurate tales of the gamblers, Victorian-era ladies of the night, and vicious criminals who shaped the Gaslamp's sordid reputation and whose restless spirits still walk the streets. San Diego's #1-Rated Tour: Join the top-rated ghost tour on all major platforms. Our passionate, local guides are master storytellers who bring the city's dark and twisted history to life right before your eyes. A Warning: Is This Tour For You? This is our most intense and graphic tour, featuring mature themes of true crime and vice. Due to its dark and sometimes disturbing content, it is strictly for adults (18+) and for those who are brave enough to confront the Gaslamp's sinister side. Ready for the Gaslamp's darkest hour? Book your tour now.

title: The Haunted Bus Tour
description: An Intimate Journey Aboard the "Coffin on Wheels" Tired of crowded walking tours? Explore San Diego's most haunted locations in comfort and style. This spine-tingling, 2-hour adventure takes you on an intimate journey walked these very streets—from famous gunslingers and notorious gamblers to the resilient pioneers and visionary leaders who shaped the American West. Exclusive Access to Heritage Park While other visitors can only peek from afar, your tour includes special access to walk among the stunning collection of historic Victorian homes in the beautiful Heritage Park, each with a unique story to tell. An Entertaining History Lesson Led by our famously engaging and historically accurate costumed guides, this tour is designed to be fun and interactive for everyone. It's a captivating performance, not a dry lecture. Is This Tour For You? This is our premier non-spooky, ney through the city's darkest history aboard our custom "Coffin on Wheels" bus, ensuring a personal and unforgettable night. What You'll Experience Exclusive Access Inside Haunted Locations: Go where other tours can't. We take you inside famously active sites, including the mysterious Villa Montezuma Mansion and the notorious Horton Grand Hotel, home to the ghost of a long-dead gambler. Expert Storytelling Across the City: Hear chilling tales of true crime and dark history from our master guides as we journey through Old Town, the historic Sherman Heights, and the sinister back alleys of the Gaslamp Quarter. A Genuine Haunting Be prepared... real paranormal events can and do happen. Guests on our tours have reported sudden cold spots, disembodied voices, and even feeling an unseen touch from a spectral hand. An Intimate, Small-Group Experience: With a maximum of 14 guests, you're guaranteed a personal and interactive tour with the best seats in the house for every spooky story. The Journey Your adventure begins in the heart of haunted Old Town at the El Campo Santo Cemetery. From there, you will board the "Coffin on Wheels" to explore the other haunted neighborhoods before being returned to your starting point. Ready to climb aboard? Book now to secure your spot... if you dare.

title: The Original Old Town Haunted Ghost Tour
description: Go Where Other Tours Can't in the Birthplace of California. Embark on a spine-chilling walking tour through the historic heart of San Diego. As darkness falls on Old Town, you'll join our master storytellers to unveil the city's sinister past, its most eerie tales, and the chilling encounters that still happen to this day. Grab your camera and your courage—you're going to need them. Exclusive Access You Won't Find Anywhere Else Enter Heritage Park After Dark: While other tours just walk by, we have the key. We are the only tour company with exclusive permission to enter this famously haunted Victorian park after the gates are locked for the night. Step Inside El Campo Santo Cemetery: Go beyond the fence and walk among the disturbed graves of Old Town's restless dead. We'll take you inside this historic cemetery, a place with a long history of the macabre where strange figures are still seen. Explore the Infamous Whaley House Grounds: Stand on the grounds of what is widely known as America's Most Haunted House. You'll hear the tragic and terrifying true stories that have made this location a nexus for paranormal activity. A Genuine Haunted Experience: This isn't just stories. Real paranormal events—from strange sounds and EMF fluctuations to shadowy figures—can and do happen on this tour. Be prepared! Is This Tour For You? This is our classic and most popular ghost tour, perfect for families (with older children), couples, and anyone curious about San Diego's most famous haunts. Due to the spooky themes, it is recommended for guests ages 10 and up. Ready to explore Old Town's haunted history? Book your tour now!

title: San Diego Tequila Tour
description: WHAT I PROVIDE for ONLY $99 pp*5 Full Shots of premium Tequila (blancos, reposados, anejos).*1 Huge Fish Taco (veggie/chicken optional).*Chips with dip and salsa.*Discuss local history and landmarks as we walk throughout the birthplace of California, Old Town San Diego.*Learn about the process of producing tequila and the different tastes barreling tequila brings out. WHAT TO BRINGPhoto IDMinimum drinking age 21HatSweaterSunglassesComfortable walking shoesCamera PICK YOUR TIMEDaily: 3pm and 6pm. (Sat & Sun also a noon start) WHAT I ALSO PROVIDE *Leave San Diego Tequila Tour with a whole new understanding and appreciation of tequila! **All guests will be emailed a Professional Google Photos link to relive their experience for a lifetime to come! The group will meet in Old Town San Diego at Rockin' Baja Lobster, a notoriously fun Mexican restaurant! We'll sip our first tequila shot while enjoying chips and a full salsa bar. The tour then takes us on a beautiful scenic walk. Guests are encouraged to stop at some of the many store's to purchase souvenirs along the way. Our next stop is high on the hillside at the Old Town Tequila Factory where we sample another shot of tequila AND enjoy a delicious fish taco on the rooftop patio with a beautiful scenic view that overlooks Downtown San Diego and the San Diego Harbor that gives that perfect backdrop for a selfie! As the tour continues, we learn about the Whaley House, early California, grave sites, historical markers, and other Old Town facts as we pass them by on our way to our final stop. Saving the best for last, the restaurant, bar and tequila museum El Agave. This is a top shelf tequileria and carries over 2, 000 tequilas. Here you'll have your OWN PERSONAL FLIGHT of tequila that is highly rated by the bar vendors while enjoying this fine establishment ending our tour.

title: The Old Town Walking History Tour
description: The Tour of Heroes: Legends of Old Town San Diego Step back in time and walk the sunlit streets of historic Old Town, the birthplace of California. This is not a ghost tour; it's a journey into the real lives, legends, and legacies of the incredible figures who built San Diego. Our passionate, costumed guides are master storytellers who bring the city's vibrant and often wild past to life right before your eyes, revealing the secrets hidden in plain sight. What You'll Experience on This Tour Meet the Heroes & Villains of the West: Hear the captivating true stories and forgotten legends of the figures who walked these very streets—from famous gunslingers and notorious gamblers to the resilient pioneers and visionary leaders who shaped the American West. Exclusive Access to Heritage Park: While other visitors can only peek from afar, your tour includes special access to walk among the stunning collection of historic Victorian homes in the beautiful Heritage Park, each with a unique story to tell. An Entertaining History Lesson: Led by our famously engaging and historically accurate costumed guides, this tour is designed to be fun and interactive for everyone. It's a captivating performance, not a dry lecture. Is This Tour For You? This is our premier non-spooky, daytime tour, perfect for families, students, history buffs, and anyone who wants to discover the fascinating true stories of Old Town San Diego. It is family-friendly and suitable for all ages. Ready to meet the legends of Old Town? Book your history tour today!

title: Summer Movies in the Park
description: Summer Movies in the Park brings communities across San Diego, CA, together for free, family-friendly outdoor movie nights at local parks. Since 2007, this popular series has offered residents the chance to enjoy blockbuster films under the stars, with pre-show activities adding to the fun before each screening. With over a hundred showings at dozens of parks, it’s a unique way to experience San Diego’s beautiful outdoor spaces after hours. Gather your friends and family, bring a blanket, and enjoy a memorable evening at your neighborhood park.

title: To Catch a Fish
description: To Catch a Fish presents over sixty works of craft and art related to fishing and the relationship between people and fish. The exhibition includes utilitarian objects such as basketry, fish traps, scrapers, weights, and nets. It also features works with spiritual significance, including amulets, robes, images of saints, and kites, along with contemporary pieces addressing the impact of commercial fisheries on Indigenous communities.

title: Prospect
description: Prospect at the Museum of Contemporary Art San Diego showcases innovative new works proposed for the museum’s collection, offering visitors a glimpse into the evolving landscape of contemporary art. Installed throughout the museum’s galleries, these works engage in conversation with MCASD’s existing collection, reflecting current curatorial priorities and artistic directions. Supported by the museum’s International and Contemporary Collectors, Prospect invites art enthusiasts to experience the future of contemporary art in a dynamic and thought-provoking setting.

title: Farm to Craft
description: Farm to Craft is an exhibition curated from the Mingei International Museum’s permanent collection that explores the global significance of grains as a source of sustenance and inspiration for human creativity. The exhibition features objects related to grains such as corn, wheat, rice, quinoa, and oats, highlighting their roles in communities and cultures worldwide. It showcases tools, containers, and artistic creations made from straw, husks, chaff, hulls, and grains, demonstrating the intersection of innovative design and craftsmanship.

title: Back From the Brink
description: How do you save a species when time is working against you? Back From the Brink tells the stories of three extraordinary animal species rescued from extinction through coordinated conservation efforts. The film, narrated by Clare Danes, highlights the work of scientists, community members, and park rangers dedicated to protecting these species.

title: Film: Ocean Paradise
description: Thousands of miles from civilization lie the keys to safeguarding our planet—three untouched atolls that give us insight into human impacts on Earth. Join us on a journey across wild tropics, pristine shores, and thriving reefs few will ever see in person. Narrated by Diane Lane and featuring music from Jack Johnson and Coldplay, Ocean Paradise explores the stories of Palmyra Atoll, Midway Atoll, and Rose Atoll Marine National Monument—remote sanctuaries across the Pacific Ocean. Research here helps scientists understand and combat climate change and large-scale pollution. Journey to the far edges of the Pacific in this hopeful tale of ecological conservation and discovery. Ocean Paradise is a Giant Screen Film production. The Giant Screen Experience is included with paid admission and is free for members.

title: Film: Back From the Brink
description: Back From the Brink tells the remarkable stories of three extraordinary animal species rescued from extinction. Discover the coordinated efforts that saved a tiny fox in California's Channel Islands, a fabled monkey in China's Yunnan Province, and the iconic Christmas Island crabs off Australia's coast. Narrated by actress Clare Danes, the film showcases conservation in action. Meet the inspiring scientists, community members, park rangers, and students working to protect these species and support their recovery. Back From the Brink is a Cosmic Pictures distribution release in association with The Nature Conservancy. Written by Kurt Frey and Jennifer Casey, the film is executive produced by Anthony and Joe Russo and directed by Sean Casey. The Giant Screen Experience is included with paid admission and is free for members.

title: San Diego Ranchos & Winery
description: Explore the rustic beauty and rich heritage of San Diego's inland ranches paired with exceptional local wines. This San Diego Ranchos and Winery Private Tour offers a unique blend of California history, scenic landscapes, and curated wine tasting experiences. Enjoy a guided journey through historic ranch lands, working farms, and boutique wineries in Ramona, Julian, or Highland Valley. Tour Includes:✅ Private round-trip transportation from San Diego✅ Professional driver and bilingual local guide✅ Visit to a historic California Rancho or working ranch property✅ Entry fees and guided history talk or ranch tour✅ Tastings at 2 local wineries in San Diego's backcountry✅ Farm-style picnic lunch or winery lunch✅ Complimentary bottled water and snacks✅ Scenic stops for photos and local insight Not Incluided:​❌ Gratuities for guide and driver (optional)❌ Premium tastings or wine purchases❌ Hotel accommodations❌ Personal expenses6 Hours | Operates: Daily upon requestDeparture: 11:00 AM | Return approx. 5:00 PMmust be 18+ to join

title: San Diego Vineyard and Winery
description: Discover the taste of Southern California's hidden wine gems.​Escape the city and immerse yourself in the rolling hills and rustic charm of San Diego's inland wine country. Our San Diego Vineyard and Winery Private Tour is a curated experience for wine lovers who want to savor local wines, meet passionate vintners, and enjoy a relaxing day among the vines—all with the ease of private transportation and your personal guide. Tour Includes:​✅ Private round-trip transportation from San Diego✅ Dedicated tour guide and driver✅ Visits to 3 hand-picked wineries in Ramona or Highland Valley Wine Regions✅ Wine tastings at each stop (5-6 tastings per winery)✅ Gourmet picnic lunch or pre-selected winery lunch✅ Complimentary bottled water✅ Winery access fees and coordination✅ Time for vineyard walks and photos Not Incluided​❌ Gratuities for guide and driver (optional but appreciated)❌ Additional bottles, purchases, or premium wine tastings❌ Hotel accommodations❌ Personal expenses6 Hours | Operates: Daily upon requestDeparture: 11:00 AM | Return approx. 5:00 PMmust be 18+ to join

title: Step-On Guide Services
description: Experience the Best of San Diego with Our Expert Tours Renowned for our intimate small group walking tours, we are thrilled to extend our expertise to larger groups as well. With over 20 years of experience, our owner Jennifer has been guiding bus tours in San Diego, delighting diverse audiences including inbound tour operators, cruise ship guests, and visitors from Los Angeles. Discover San Diego with Jennifer, who will captivate your group with engaging stories and insightful commentary about the city's rich history, vibrant culture, and iconic landmarks. Each tour is customized to your group's unique interests, ensuring a memorable experience tailored to your schedule. Choose our general city highlights tour for a comprehensive overview of San Diego's top attractions, or collaborate with us to create a custom tour suited to your specific preferences. Bring Jennifer's expertise to your location with specialized talks about San Diego, available upon request. City Highlights can include: Old Town - original location of Spanish settlement with Adobe buildings and a haunted house Balboa Park - best urban park in the U.S. filled with amazing architecture and gorgeous gardens Coronado - quaint beach town home to both Naval Station North Island and the historic Hotel del Coronado La Jolla - "the jewel" is renowned for its quaint village, rocky shoreline, and abundant sea life Downtown/Gaslamp - historic heart of San Diego home to both Victorian architecture and the San Diego Padres baseball stadium Embarcadero - historic waterfront full of museums, shops, eateries, and great views of passing ships Barrio Logan - Mexican American neighborhood containing historic murals, eateries, and art centers celebrating Chicano history and culture

title: Baja Mexico Vineyard and Winery
description: Embark on an unforgettable wine journey through Valle de Guadalupe, Baja California's premier wine region. Just a short drive from San Diego, this private tour offers a deep dive into the rustic beauty, world-class wines, and bold culinary experiences that make this area one of Latin America's most talked-about destinations.Let us guide you through boutique wineries, scenic vineyards, and gourmet food spots—all curated to deliver the ultimate Baja wine experience. Tour Includes:​​​✅ Private round-trip transportation from San Diego✅ Visit to 3 carefully selected wineries in Valle de Guadalupe✅ Premium wine tastings at each winery✅ Gourmet farm-to-table lunch with Baja Med flavors✅ Scenic drive through Baja's Wine Route✅ English-speaking tour host or driver✅ Bottled water and snacks onboard✅ All taxes and coordination fees Not Included:​❌ Gratuities for driver or guide❌ Additional bottles of wine or souvenir purchases❌ Hotel accommodations (optional add-on)9 Hours | Operates: Daily upon requestDeparture: 9 AM | Return approx. 6:00 PMPrivate tour — includes multiple tastings and Baja-style lunch | Must be 21+ to join

title: Elephant Valley Now Open
description: Introducing Denny Sanford Elephant Valley at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park Step into a world where you can roam freely with the largest land animals on Earth. Experience what it feels like to coexist with them. To become one with the herd. To be more than just a passive witness to these incredible creatures. Here, you'll walk beside them. Together, on common ground. Because it takes standing by them for you to truly be inspired to stand with them. And become a part of a movement that helps protect them for generations to come. This is Elephant Valley. A space that will bring us together like never before. A place where together, we roam. Now open since March 5th, 2026

title: Temecula Vineyard & Winery Private Tour
description: Embark on a full-day private tour through the rolling hills and sun-kissed vineyards of Temecula Valley. Known for its award-winning wines, boutique wineries, and countryside charm, Temecula is the perfect escape for wine lovers, couples, or groups seeking a curated and intimate experience. Sit back, relax, and let us take care of the driving as you sip, savor, and soak in the beauty of wine country.What's included Private round-trip transportation from San Diego Visits to 3 hand-selected wineries (customized to your preferences) Wine tastings at each location Scenic countryside drive through Temecula Wine Country Optional gourmet picnic lunch or winery restaurant reservation Bottled water and snacks onboard Professional driver or host All taxes, tolls, and coordination feesHighlights Private, comfortable, round-trip transportation Visits to three boutique wineries in Temecula Personalized wine tasting experiences Scenic views and countryside relaxation Optional vineyard lunch or picnic experience Perfect for couples, birthdays, corporate outings or bachelorette groups

title: Senior Mondays
description: Senior Mondays is a monthly event held on the first Monday of each month, featuring an engaging lecture on scientific topics followed by a show in the Heikoff Giant Dome Theater. The event offers discounted admission for seniors and includes interactive experiences. Tickets are available only at the ticket counter on the day of the event, with free access to the Sharp Minds lecture for Fleet members.

title: Meditation
description: This meditation session is designed for adults to immerse themselves in relaxation and tranquility through guided techniques such as breathwork, visualization, and mindfulness exercises. The program is led by Hema Singh and/or Sheetal Gupta, both experienced practitioners with backgrounds in leading meditation sessions and participating in mental health campaigns. The event provides a dedicated adult-only space for enhancing overall well-being.

title: The Cagematch
description: The Cagematch is an improv comedy event where two teams compete on stage with quick wit and improvisational skills. The audience participates by deciding which team advances to the next showdown through their reactions. The event is designed as an interactive and energetic improv battle.

title: Stay Sharp with Games
description: This event focuses on preserving mental abilities through stimulating activities such as playing games and learning new skills. It is designed to help keep the brain healthy as people age. The program is held on the second and fourth Mondays of each month and welcomes adults of all ages.

title: Ensenada Pacific Coastline Tour from San Diego
description: Discover the coastal beauty and cultural charm of Ensenada on this full-day adventure from San Diego. From the bustling seafood black market to oceanfront promenades and colorful local shops, this experience captures the heart of Baja California's most beloved port city. Ideal for travelers seeking a relaxed yet immersive cultural escape, this tour offers a perfect blend of flavors, history, and scenic ocean views. Tour Highlights: Scenic drive along the Pacific Coast from San Diego to Ensenada Visit the famous Seafood Black Market (Mercado Negro) for fresh local catches Stroll along El Malecón, Ensenada's iconic seaside promenade Explore La Calle Primera, filled with local shops, handicrafts, and souvenirs Free time for local shopping and optional lunch at a traditional seafood restaurant Border-crossing assistance and bilingual guide throughout the tour Ensenada Pacific Coastline Tour from San DiegoDuration: 10-12 hours Includes:​​​ Round-trip private transportation from San Diego Bilingual tour guide Guided visit to main landmarks and local markets Bottled water on board ​Not Included:​​​​​ Meals and drinks Personal purchases or souvenirs Gratuities for guide and driver

title: San Diego Chasing Sunsets Tour
description: Discover San Diego's breathtaking beauty during golden hour on our exclusive Chasing Sunsets Tour—a 4-hour journey along the Pacific Coast designed to capture the most magical moments of the day. Begin in a picturesque seaside town such as La Jolla or Ocean Beach, and let our expert local guide take you through a curated route of San Diego's top coastal viewpoints. Stops:​ Sunset Cliffs Natural Park - dramatic cliffs and crashing waves Mt. Soledad - sweeping panoramic views of the entire city Windansea Beach - a local favorite with textured rock formations Secret coastal parks and off-the-beaten-path lookouts 3 Hours |Includes: guide, view points, wine and olive oil tasting stop and viewpoints. Includes:​ Private round-trip transportation from your location Local San Diego guide Stops at 3-4 top sunset viewpoints Bottled water and light snacks Time for walking, exploring, and photo opportunities ​Not Included: Gratuities Meals unless picnic upgrade is selected Personal expenses

title: Tinker Tuesdays
description: Tinker Tuesdays is a recurring program held at the Mission Hills-Hillcrest Library, where participants can bring their ideas to life using various maker lab equipment. The event features tools such as 3D printers, a laser cutter, and a die-cut machine powered by Silhouette Studio. It is open to all ages, with some equipment requiring an appointment.

title: California Tower Tours
description: Join the Museum of Us for a uniquely-San Diego experience! Built in 1915, the California Tower is one of San Diego's most iconic historic landmarks. Make the climb on a guided tour that highlights the origins of Balboa Park and the histories of some of the many diverse communities that call the region home. All tours culminate in breathtaking 360-degree views of San Diego from the Tower's eighth-floor viewing deck—from the Pacific Ocean in the west to the Cuyamaca Mountains in the east, and beyond. California Tower tours are offered daily when the Museum is open, per the schedule below: Monday-Thursday: 10:30 a.m., 12:30 p.m., 2:30 p.m. and 4:30 p.m. Friday-Sunday: Hourly from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m.

title: Trivia
description: Trivia at Brewery X is a weekly trivia night event held at Brewery X, located on Harbor Island in San Diego. Participants can test their knowledge and compete for prizes. The event occurs every Wednesday evening from 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM throughout most of 2026.

title: Liberty Station: From Barracks to Brews
description: Calling all new recruits! Join a local guide and get an inside look at what it was like to join the navy without a mulit-year commitment! Learn some sailor skills, explore the buildings essential to training, learn fun facts about the recruit experience, and gain an understanding of the history of the Naval Training Center. Tour Highlights Learn important sailor skills without having to drop and give us 20 Explore recruit life by visiting buildings essential to training such as barracks, the mess hall, and other facilities Using historic photos, we'll compare the recruit experience with the modern-day visitor's experience Celebrate the completion of your "training" by raising a brew (or comparable beverage) PRIVATE TOURS AVAILABLE; CLICK "TICKETS" FOR DETAILS Itinerary We will kick things off by exploring the heart of what was once Camp Paul Jones, the first camp established that housed 100-150 recruits per barracks and get a feel for what it was like to be one of those sailors (without the drill instructor!). Its park-like setting is full of secrets - such as the history of the large tree there and the special requirements for decorating it at Christmas, the design of the barracks' roof that's key to keeping it cool, or the connection the current public art on display has with the camp's Navy past. Then pass by the former dispensary, where early recruits received medical care - sometimes for the first time in their lives. We'll view the former Sail Ho Golf Course because it wasn't all work and no play on the base. Several golf pros have played here, and the course is even a final resting place for two people! At the historic entrance, we get a feel for what it was like to enter the base as a new recruit, hear how the gate played a role in their daily lives, and what impact having the Marine Core Training Center had on Naval recruits. It wasn't all men who served here, at least not after 1943 when WAVES (Women Accepted for Volunteer Emergency Service) arrived, taking up residence in a nearby barracks away from the men. We'll then pass by the very first buildings on base which contained the Mess Hall and talk about what it took to feed so many people, multiple times a day. We'll pause at a work of art that replicates the whale boat sailors learned to row in the nearby channel and learn a sailor skill or two. Afterward we'll head over to the heart of the base - the Command Center - where the top brass kept the base running. There we'll view an exhibit that highlights recruit life including something dear to everyone - food! From there it's on to Ingram Plaza, to learn about the person it was named for, and why that's a better name than its alternative. Next up we'll visit the Boats Memorial, dedicated to the 52 submarines and their crews lost during World War II. If time and energy allow, we'll walk to the landlocked ship referred to by locals as the "USS Neversail." Then we'll proceed across Preble Field, where recruits marched during training and on their graduation day. We'll celebrate their legacy and your "graduation" from the tour and sailor training with a beverage near where we started. From there, your guide will recommend additional ways to explore the Navy's legacy at Liberty Station, along with how to enjoy the area's many cultural organizations or provide information on its many restaurants.

title: Secrets and Highlights of Balboa Park
description: Balboa Park is more than just a beautiful place — it's a living museum of San Diego's story. Join us for a walk filled with art, architecture, and nature, all brought to life with fun and surprising details. Tour Highlights Discover the inspiring, quirky, and fascinating history of one of the nation's largest urban cultural parksUncover historical and architectural treasures hiding in plain sightExplore a botanical garden's vibrant collection.Learn about the early 20th century expositions that shaped Balboa ParkFind the best spots to photograph Balboa Park's beautiful Spanish colonial revival architecture PRIVATE TOURS AND TEAM BUILDING SCAVENGER HUNTS ARE AVAILABLE. CLICK "TICKETS" FOR DETAILS Tour Itinerary Balboa Park is home to gorgeous gardens, lovely walking paths, and plenty of cultural gems, from theaters to museums featuring the arts, the sciences, and more. You get a full overview of the park and its rich history on this San Diego walking tour that explores how the park has become a symbol of the city. We kick things off at the Founder's Statue on the west side of the park. We think you'll agree San Diegans were visionaries when they set aside 1,400 acres of land for the park all the way back in 1868. Then, we stop at the California Tower, built for the 1915 Panama-California Exposition, which has since become a city landmark. The face of the tower depicts the early history of the city. Next up, we head to Alcazar Gardens, which was inspired by the same-named gardens in Spain. Used for both the 1915 and 1935 expositions, the garden offered fair-goers a respite from the hustle and bustle of the exposition and now provides a picturesque foreground to photos of the California Tower. Snag the perfect Instagram shot while you're here. Following that, we head to the Old Globe Theater which is a Tony Award-winning, world-renowned professional theater group modeled after The Globe Theatre London. From there, we walk through the heart of the Prado, where you get to spy unique building façades and roof supports that most visitors don't notice. We also check out Zoro Gardens, a sunken garden that was once a nudist colony (seriously) during the 1935 California Pacific International Exposition. Today, it's a butterfly garden that contains everything butterflies need for their full life cycle. We then continue past the Bea Evenson fountain, the Natural History Museum, and we pop into the Spanish Village Art Center to observe artists at work in their studios. By now, you should have worked up a thirst so we grab a drink at a local coffee cart before continuing on past one of the largest Morton Bay fig trees in California and the San Diego Junior Theater. But we're not done yet! We then head to the Botanical Building. It's the perfect stop for photographers and flower-lovers alike, and the adjacent lily pond full of koi makes more than just a pretty picture — you learn how the pond was an important part of the war effort during WWI and how it helped injured sailors and soldiers during WWII. Our tour ends at the Plaza de Panama, but before we say goodbye, your guide gives you directions to the nearby Visitor Center (for snacks, maps, souvenirs, and museum passes), suggestions for places to eat, and tips on what else to see and do within the park.

title: San Diego City and Coastline Daily Tour
description: Perfect for first-time visitors and curious locals alike, our San Diego Highlights Daily Tour is a personalized 4-hour journey showcasing the best of America's Finest City. Travel in comfort with your own private driver-guide as you explore San Diego's most iconic neighborhoods and breathtaking coastal views.Begin in the historic Gaslamp Quarter, known for its Victorian-era architecture and vibrant nightlife. Cruise past Petco Park and the modern skyline before heading to Balboa Park, home to over 16 museums, lush gardens, and the world-famous San Diego Zoo.​Cross the scenic Coronado Bridge to explore the charming island of Coronado, where you'll admire the iconic Hotel del Coronado and white-sand beaches. Then, journey north along the coast to the upscale seaside village of La Jolla, where you'll spot sea lions basking on the rocks and stop at the panoramic Mt. Soledad National Veterans Memorial, offering sweeping 360° views of the Pacific Ocean, San Diego skyline, and even Mexico on clear days.

title: Old Town Tales, Tacos and Tequila
description: Step into the birthplace of California with a tour that blends rich history, authentic street food, and tequila! From historic landmarks to handmade tortillas, you'll savor the spirit (and the spirits) of Old Town. Tour Highlights Explore San Diego's most historic neighborhood Learn about the indigenous people who called San Diego home thousands of years before the arrival of European settlers Find out how life changes once the Americans arrive and establish a new government Choose from a vast selection of tequila varieties at an authentic tequileria that boasts a collection of thousands in its museum End your experience enjoying delicious gourmet street tacos Private version of this tour also available. Itinerary As the birthplace of California, Old Town San Diego has seen its fair share of history. Today, it's also widely regarded for its restaurants, margaritas, and souvenir shops. And we'll get to sample a bit of all of these on this 2.5 hour walking tour! We'll start our exploration at the Adobe Chapel which served both as a home and church in the 19th century. Then you'll be treated to handmade tortillas, a world-famous dish that dates back to before Columbus landed in North America. We'll continue the tour and view one of the oldest brick buildings in Southern California, best known for the ghosts who haunt it and see some wonderful examples of Victorian architecture, including San Diego's first synagogue. View the hill where the Spanish settled and learn about the indigenousness people who were here when the Europeans arrived. We'll visit a hundred-year-old Catholic church, followed by a stroll through a landmark cemetery with graves dating back to the 1850s. By now you might be thirsty, so we will take a brief walk to a local tequileria boasting a collection of thousands of bottles. Here you will learn about the many different types of tequilas and be treated to a taste of one of the best tequilas in the world. (This sample is included as part of your tour price, but you may instead choose a margarita, beer or another beverage of your choice). We'll bid adios here, but feel free to linger over another drink, or dig into some great Mexican food. Or, if you want to keep moving, you can continue shopping and exploring this great San Diego neighborhood.

title: Coronado History and Highlights
description: Get off the beaten path and uncover the stories behind Coronado's beautiful homes, historic streets, and seaside splendor. It's a perfect blend of California history, architecture, and coastal vibes. Tour Highlights Discover the hidden history of Coronado — from Victorian elegance to seaside beauty! Marvel at the historic Hotel del Coronado, where celebs and presidents have laid their heads Enjoy a delicious sweet treat at our favorite local eatery Hear stories of the artists, aviators and innovators who made the island their home. PRIVATE TOURS ALSO AVAILABLE. CLICK ON "TICKETS" FOR DETAILS Itinerary A trip to Coronado feels like you're leaving behind the mainland for a dose of island living, but it's not your typical beach town. Part resort, part naval base, part luxury homes for the rich, Coronado is many things to many people. We'll meet at Rotary park on Orange Avenue, the main street in town named after the orange trees planted here in the late 19th century. This main part of town is lined with cafes, shops, and restaurants, almost all locally-owned and operated. We'll work our way down to the public library, the original building donated by John Spreckels. Then we'll get off the main drag and explore some lesser known areas, full of architecture ranging from Gothic to Modern. After a quick sweet treat we'll take a stroll down to Star Park, where the "Wizard of Oz" house is located and then make our way to Coronado beach, one of the best in the country. On a clear day, you can see all the way to Mexico! We stroll by multi-million dollar homes that line Ocean Boulevard and you hear about the people who once lived there, including the residents of the historic Spreckels Mansion. After getting a look at the historic Hotel del Coronado, an 135+ year old icon and American treasure, having hosted celebrities, dignitaries, and US presidents, we'll stroll back to where we started, with a brief stop in one of our favorite shops before we say our goodbyes. Your guide will make suggestions on what else to see and do, and gives you tips on where to eat and shop.

title: Flavors of Little Italy: Food Tour
description: No San Diego Little Italy walking tour would be complete without exploring this culinary hotspot's best places to eat. Known for its variety of restaurants, America's biggest Little Italy has become a food lover's bucket list destination, and we're here to show it off! Join our Little Italy walking tour full of food, fun, and Italian culture. Little Italy is an Italian enclave of great culinary delights in downtown San Diego, and its vibrant atmosphere can be observed during a city tour. San Diego's Italian quarter has grown in popularity for not just Italian fare, but also for the rich array of local restaurant hot-spots that have opened up in the last 10 years, or so. In addition to the traditional pasta and pizza dishes, Little Italy offers up top chef cuisine, vegan breakfasts, juicy burgers, and lounges for dancing and fine cocktails! Consider every day a Little Italy food fest! The streets of Little Italy are lined with Italian American staples, making the Little Italy Tour a must-try experience among travelers visiting San Diego. The possibilities to whet your palate are endless. After all, what better way to savor the delicious food paired with sangria, beer, or wine tasting. Our Little Italy tours will have you savoring iconic bites while you get an understanding of the history and current vibe of the neighborhood with one of our professional and personable tour guides. A walking tour of the exciting history of Little Italy Taste a combination of Italian flavors and unique bites from 4 restaurants that represent the area Learn about the early fishing industry of San Diego Meet some of the local Italians and Italian immigrants who've been a part of the revitalization of the area Wine, sangria or local beer tasters Days/Times Available*: Monday through Thursday at 4:30pm Rate (Age 13+): $88 per Person Duration: 2.5 Hours Minimum Guests: 4 / Maximum Guests: 15

title: ALL-NEW Shark Encounter
description: ALL-NEW Shark Encounter to Open May 22, 2026 Come face-to-fin with some of the ocean's most fascinating predators in a newly refreshed exhibit: Encounter mesmerizing new shark species alongside a vibrant array of marine life—including additional sharks and colorful fish—as the expanded exhibit transforms into a dynamic underwater adventure. Experience the ocean like never before in a breathtakingly enhanced space that pulls guests into an immersive realm—celebrating the beauty and power of sharks while challenging long-held myths about this extraordinary species. The all-new Shark Encounter steps beyond traditional fear-based portrayals to educate guests about the incredible diversity of sharks—over 400 species worldwide—highlighting their varied sizes, shapes, and fascinating adaptations that contribute to healthy oceans. This is more than a refresh—it's a commitment: The reimagined experience continues to showcase the stunning array of sharks, with 11 different species including sand tiger sharks, blacktip reef sharks, and the endangered Australian leopard shark—part of a Species Survival Plan through AZA-accredited facilities. Almost all the species at Shark Encounter are listed as at least vulnerable or near threatened, reinforcing SeaWorld's commitment to education and species protection. Stunning visual upgrades and immersive storytelling: State-of-the-art multimedia brings the new experience to life, fusing striking visuals with education-driven storytelling to deepen the connection between humans and one of the ocean's most iconic predators. An overhead video ceiling in the entry hallway features sharks swimming above, guiding guests toward the underwater tunnel. In the finale, a multi-screen installation surrounds you with rare live footage of shark diversity in a mesmerizing 8-minute presentation. Exclusive VIP Shark Tour: As part of the all-new experience, adventurers will have the chance to learn up-close how our zoological specialists feed and care for the sharks. The tour includes access to a brand-new walkway above the shark habitat, offering a dramatic, elevated view of these fascinating animals from a unique perspective. This unforgettable experience promotes deeper understanding, personal connection, and marine life conservation. Purchase details coming soon!

title: Petco Park Tours
description: Experience Petco Park like never before with a behind-the-scenes ballpark tour! Guests will have the opportunity to explore exclusive areas of the stadium, learn about the history of the San Diego Padres, and take in one of baseball's most iconic venues from a unique perspective. Tours are available on select dates throughout the season; however, tours do not operate on certain event days, holidays, and select game or stadium blackout dates. On concert days, tour times may be adjusted to accommodate event operations. Tours are currently not available on the following dates. This list is subject to change. 2026: March 7 (San Diego Science Expo) March 21-22 (Savannah Bananas) April 30 (Full Ballpark Event) May 13-16 (Full Ballpark Event) June 11-14 (Field of Dreamz Festival) June 16-19 (Fuerza Rediga) July 19-22 (Ed Sheeran Concert) August 13-16 (Rufus Du Sol) August 15-18 (Noah Kahan) August 28-31 (My Chemical Romance Concert) September 10-13 (Pierce the Veil) On Gallagher Square concerts days, we adjust our tour schedule as follows: Weekday: The 2:00 PM tour will not be available Weekends: The 1:00 PM and 2:00 PM tours will not be available Please refer to the Petco Park event schedule to view upcoming concert dates. For the most up-to-date tour schedule, blackout dates, and adjusted concert tour times, please visit the Padres Tours page.

title: Fight Inflation on This Elevated Wine Tasting
description: This event offers a VIP wine tasting experience at a low price, including door-to-door transportation, ample wine pours, a wine tour, and a winery meal. It features local San Diego County wineries and provides a fully guided adventure with entertaining commentary on local history and viticulture. The tours run from Wednesday through Sunday for groups ranging from 2 to 100 guests, welcoming single guests as well.

title: San Diego Beer, Wine, and Spirits
description: Head to the original California wine country for a memorable tasting experience. Explore the natural beauty of the vineyards while savoring the unique flavors of the region's world-class wines.

title: Klara Chavarria Art Gallery
description: The Klara Chavarria Art Gallery showcases the captivating work of artist Klara Chavarría, featuring a vibrant collection of internationally acclaimed abstract and figurative expressionism. The gallery offers a profound exploration of human emotions and existence through contemporary fine art. The event recurs monthly on the first Friday through January 1, 2027, providing an ongoing opportunity to experience Klara's unique artistic vision.

title: Shelter Island Farmers Market
description: Shelter Island Friday Farmers Market is a weekly waterfront market held every Friday at the Island Palms Hotel and Marina. The market features local artisans, makers, and small businesses offering fresh sourdough, honey, desserts, clothing, skincare, resin art, hats, bags, hot tea, and more. Visitors can enjoy the market's laid-back atmosphere with beautiful Marina views and island vibes while supporting local vendors.

title: LEGO® Galaxy NOW OPEN at LEGOLAND® California
description: LEGO® Galaxy is an ALL-NEW space-themed land at LEGOLAND® California where families can launch into a world of imagination, creativity, and adventure inspired by decades of LEGO space stories. Guests can explore an immersive environment filled with impressive brick built space models, interactive experiences, and character meet and greets. The land brings together classic and modern LEGO space themes, inviting explorers of all ages to discover distant worlds, train like astronauts, and see LEGO creativity taken to a whole new universe. At the heart of LEGO Galaxy is a collection of adventures designed for the whole family, including Galacticoaster, an indoor family coaster, along with additional space inspired rides and a dedicated toddler play zone. Guests can build and test LEGO rockets, enjoy hands on play areas, and refuel with galactic themed food and treats before stopping by Orbital Outpost to pick up space ready souvenirs.
